principle_scores.rationale: Opportunity directly aligns with Envision's logistics and sustainment strategic focus. Army customer is within target customer set.
Standard government contract with no ethical concerns. Full and open competition ensures fair pursuit.
Potential teaming with Riverstone (HUBZone) strengthens bid but requires coordination. Cross-OU collaboration opportunity.
Sufficient FPDS and SAM.gov data to make evidence-based pursuit decision. Some gaps in incumbent performance data.
45-day response timeline is executable within 90-day milestone framework. Adequate time for Shipley process.
Strengthens existing Army Sustainment Command positioning. Builds on RS3 relationship but extends to new PEO.
Within Envision's lane but slightly adjacent to core C5ISR focus. Logistics sustainment is core capability.

output.alignment_summary: Strong doctrine alignment at 82/100. Opportunity fits squarely within Envision's logistics and sustainment wheelhouse with existing Army relationships as a foundation. Minor concerns around new PEO relationship building and CMMI certification timeline.
concerns: CMMI-DEV ML3 certification expiring during potential performance period
Limited direct experience with this specific PEO
Requires investment in transition planning for workforce
